step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the assessment provided in Round 1. One additional point supporting this view is that while all these enzymes can be useful indicators, their varying durations of detectability make them suitable for different stages of diagnostic evaluation. Specifically, because LDH remains elevated longer due to its slower metabolism within cells affected by ischemia-reperfusion injuries characteristic of an MI, it serves as a valuable late-stage marker confirming ongoing tissue damage even after more acute markers have normalized.
```plaintext
Agree. Additional support comes from the fact that LDH's prolonged elevation makes it particularly useful as a confirmatory marker indicating continued tissue damage beyond what earlier markers might show.
